entries.title: Maclaurin Series
entries.content: A Taylor series expansion of a function about 0.
entries.example: Maclaurin series for e^x is 1 + x + x^2/2 + ...

entries.title: Magic Square
entries.content: A square grid of numbers where the sum of numbers in each row, column, and diagonal is the same.
entries.example: A 3x3 grid with numbers 1 to 9 having magic constant 15

entries.title: Magnitude of Vector
entries.content: The length of a vector in Euclidean space.
entries.example: Magnitude of [3, 4] is 5

entries.title: Major Axis of Ellipse
entries.content: The longest diameter of an ellipse, passing through its center and foci.
entries.example: Major axis length of x^2/9 + y^2/4 = 1 is 6

entries.title: Mandelbrot Set
entries.content: A famous fractal set of complex numbers that do not diverge under iteration of a quadratic formula.
entries.formula: z_(n+1) = z_n^2 + c remains bounded starting at z_0=0
entries.example: Plotting complex boundaries of Mandelbrot fractal

entries.title: Marginal Probability
entries.content: The probability of a single event occurring, without regard to other events.
entries.formula: P(A) = sum of P(A and B_i) over all i
entries.example: Probability of drawing a King regardless of suit

entries.title: Markov Chain
entries.content: A stochastic process where the probability of transitioning to a future state depends only on the current state.
entries.example: Weather forecasting based only on today's weather

entries.title: Markov's Inequality
entries.content: A theorem giving an upper bound for the probability that a non-negative random variable is greater than a constant.
entries.example: Bounding probability of extreme salary levels

entries.title: Mathematical Induction
entries.content: A method of mathematical proof used to establish that a statement holds for all natural numbers.
entries.formula: P(1) is true and P(k)=>P(k+1) implies P(n) is true
entries.example: Proving the sum formula for first n integers

entries.title: Matrix Addition
entries.content: Adding two matrices of identical dimensions by adding corresponding elements.
entries.example: Adding two 2x2 matrices

entries.title: Matrix Adjugate
entries.content: The transpose of the cofactor matrix of a square matrix.
entries.example: Using adjugate to compute inverse matrix: A^-1 = adj(A)/det(A)

entries.title: Matrix Determinant
entries.content: A scalar value representing scaling factor of matrix transformation.
entries.example: det of diagonal matrix is product of diagonal elements

entries.title: Matrix Dimension
entries.content: The number of rows and columns in a matrix.
entries.example: A matrix with 3 rows and 5 columns has dimensions 3x5

entries.title: Matrix Inverse
entries.content: A matrix that yields the identity matrix when multiplied with original matrix.
entries.example: Calculating inverse to solve systems of equations

entries.title: Matrix Multiplication
entries.content: Multiplying two matrices by taking dot products of rows and columns.
entries.example: Multiplying a 2x3 matrix by a 3x2 matrix to get a 2x2 matrix

entries.title: Matrix Rank
entries.content: The maximum number of linearly independent rows or columns in a matrix.
entries.example: Rank of identity matrix I_3 is 3

entries.title: Matrix Transpose
entries.content: Flipping a matrix over its diagonal, switching row and column indices.
entries.example: Transposing a 2x3 matrix into a 3x2 matrix

entries.title: Maximum Likelihood Estimation
entries.content: A method of estimating parameters of a probability distribution by maximizing a likelihood function.
entries.example: Estimating mean of normal distribution from sample data

entries.title: Maximum Value of Function
entries.content: The largest output value of a function on an interval.
entries.example: Maximum of y = -x^2 + 4 is 4 at x = 0

entries.title: Mean Absolute Deviation
entries.content: The average of the absolute differences between each data point and the mean.
entries.example: Evaluating dispersion of student test grades

entries.title: Mean Value Theorem
entries.content: Theorem stating that for a differentiable curve, there is a point where the tangent is parallel to the secant.
entries.example: At some point on a trip, instantaneous speed equals average speed

entries.title: Measure Theory
entries.content: A systematic way to assign a size or volume to subsets of a space, generalizing length, area, and volume.
entries.example: Lebesgue measure on real numbers

entries.title: Median of Data
entries.content: The middle value of an ordered dataset, separating the higher half from the lower half.
entries.example: Median of 3, 5, 20 is 5

entries.title: Median of Triangle
entries.content: A line segment joining a vertex of a triangle to the midpoint of the opposite side.
entries.example: A triangle has three medians meeting at centroid

entries.title: Metric Space
entries.content: A set equipped with a metric (distance function) defining distance between any two elements.
entries.example: Euclidean space with standard distance metric

entries.title: Midpoint Formula in 2D
entries.content: Formula calculating coordinates of the center point between two endpoints in a plane.
entries.example: Midpoint between (1, 2) and (5, 8) is (3, 5)

entries.title: Midpoint Formula in 3D
entries.content: Formula calculating coordinates of the center point between two endpoints in 3D space.
entries.example: Midpoint between (0,0,0) and (2,4,6) is (1,2,3)

entries.title: Midpoint of Segment
entries.content: The point dividing a line segment into two congruent halves.
entries.example: Finding center of a diagonal line segment

entries.title: Minor Axis of Ellipse
entries.content: The shortest diameter of an ellipse, perpendicular to the major axis.
entries.example: Minor axis length of x^2/9 + y^2/4 = 1 is 4

entries.title: Minimax Theorem
entries.content: A theorem in game theory showing that for zero-sum games, minimax value equals maximin value.
entries.formula: min max f(x, y) = max min f(x, y)
entries.example: Finding optimal minimax strategy in rock-paper-scissors

entries.title: Minimum Value of Function
entries.content: The smallest output value of a function on an interval.
entries.example: Minimum of y = x^2 is 0 at x = 0

entries.title: Minor of Matrix Element
entries.content: The determinant of the submatrix left after deleting the row and column of that element.
entries.example: Finding minor of top-left element in 3x3 matrix

entries.title: Mixed Numbers
entries.content: A number representation containing a whole number part and a fraction part.
entries.example: 2 1/2 is mixed number representing 2.5

entries.title: Mode of Data Set
entries.content: The value that appears most frequently in a dataset.
entries.example: Mode of 1, 2, 2, 3, 5 is 2

entries.title: Modular Arithmetic
entries.content: A system of arithmetic for integers where numbers wrap around after reaching a certain value (the modulus).
entries.example: 14 modulo 12 equals 2 (like clock time)

entries.title: Monoid Definition
entries.content: An algebraic structure with an associative binary operation and an identity element.
entries.example: Natural numbers under addition with 0 is a monoid

entries.title: Monomial Definition
entries.content: An algebraic expression consisting of only one term.
entries.example: 4x^3 is a monomial

entries.title: Monotonic Sequence
entries.content: A sequence of numbers that either never increases or never decreases.
entries.example: Sequence 1 - 1/n is monotonically increasing

entries.title: Multinomial Coefficient
entries.content: The number of ways to partition n distinct objects into k groups of size n_i.
entries.example: Finding coefficient of x^2 * y * z in expansion

entries.title: Multinomial Theorem
entries.content: Formula describing how to expand powers of a sum of three or more terms.
entries.example: Expanding (x + y + z)^3 algebraically
